claimText: China successfully achieved the first-ever thorium to uranium fuel conversion in a Thorium Molten Salt Reactor (TMSR).
evidenceExcerpt: China successfully achieved the first-ever thorium to uranium fuel conversion in a Thorium Molten Salt Reactor (TMSR).
examUse: Statement-based question on recent global nuclear technology milestones.
distractorHooks: India achieved the first thorium to uranium conversion
USA operates the only current TMSR

claimText: The Thorium Molten Salt Reactor (TMSR) is a fourth-generation advanced nuclear energy system that uses high-temperature molten salt as a coolant.
evidenceExcerpt: It is fourth-generation advanced nuclear energy system that uses high-temperature molten salt as a coolant.
examUse: Feature-based question on nuclear reactor generations and coolants.
distractorHooks: TMSR uses heavy water as a coolant
TMSR is a third-generation nuclear reactor

claimText: Thorium Molten Salt Reactors feature passive safety mechanisms, such as a salt plug that melts to stop the reaction if the reactor overheats.
evidenceExcerpt: Passive Safety Features: If an MSR is too hot, a salt plug melts, stopping the reaction.
examUse: Statement-based question on reactor safety mechanisms.
distractorHooks: Requires active water pumping to stop the reaction
The salt plug solidifies to contain the reaction during overheating

claimText: Thorium-232 is the only naturally occurring isotope of thorium and must be converted to Uranium-233 in a reactor before it can be used as nuclear fuel.
evidenceExcerpt: Thorium has to be converted to Uranium-233 in a reactor before it can be used as fuel. Thorium-232 is the only naturally occurring isotope of thorium.
examUse: Statement-based question on the thorium nuclear fuel cycle.
distractorHooks: Thorium-232 is directly fissile and used as fuel
Thorium is converted to Plutonium-239 for fuel
Multiple naturally occurring isotopes of thorium exist

claimText: India holds the world's largest thorium reserves, which are primarily found in monazite sands in states like Kerala and Odisha.
evidenceExcerpt: India has the world's largest reserves of thorium. Kerala and Odisha have rich reserves of monazite, which contains about 8 - 10% thorium.
distractorHooks: Australia holds the world's largest thorium reserves
Thorium in India is primarily extracted from pitchblende ores

claimText: India is developing the Advanced Heavy Water Reactor (AHWR) and Indian Molten Salt Breeder Reactor (IMSBR) for the thorium fuel cycle.
evidenceExcerpt: India is developing the Advanced Heavy Water Reactor (AHWR) and Indian Molten Salt Breeder Reactor (IMSBR) for the thorium fuel cycle.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on India's three-stage nuclear program.
distractorHooks: AHWR is designed exclusively for enriched uranium fuel
IMSBR is a light water reactor imported from Russia
